results.title: NCINGA and AWS Convene National Leaders to Architect Sri Lanka’s Sovereign Digital Future at Inaugural ‘GROUNDED’ Symposium
results.description: Global technology solutions provider NCINGA, in partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS), convened over 100 of the nation’s top government and enterprise leaders at the inaugural “GROUNDED: Sovereign Cloud Symposium” on June 17. The event introduced AWS Outposts, a hybrid cloud solution that brings the full power of AWS securely within Sri Lanka’s borders, finally aligning rapid digital transformation with stringent data residency and security requirements. Signalling a shift from basic cloud adoption to strategic architecture, the symposium reinforced a foundational truth: successful digital nations combine Global Innovation with National Ownership and Local Capability. Leaders emphasised that a nation cannot simply procure digital excellence off a shelf; it must be architected with sovereign intent and built with homegrown talent to ensure economic resilience and secure public service delivery. Setting the national agenda, Dr. Hans Wijayasuriya, Chief Advisor to His Excellency the President of Sri Lanka on Digital Economy, framed the discussion within the government’s audacious goal to grow the digital economy by 5X to over 15 billion by 2030. He outlined the country’s core technology principles of Cloud−First, AI−First, and a primacy for Horizontal Systems, designed to break down data silos and speed up the delivery of citizen services. He specifically recognised hybrid models like AWS Outposts as the “best of both worlds,” bringing global−scale innovation into a secure, resident environment to unlock a $25 billion value creation opportunity. Endorsing the state’s commitment, Hon. Eng. Eranga Weeraratne, Deputy Minister to the Digital Economy, positioned digitalisation as the critical path for Sri Lanka to “leapfrog” its economic development. He detailed the government’s formal Cloud-First policy and expressed a clear national ambition for Sri Lanka to become a regional data centre hub. The symposium’s central panel discussion, moderated by Vajira Wijesinghe, Group MD of NCINGA, translated high-level strategy into executable action. Industry leaders Pete Herlihy (Digital Public Infrastructure Lead, AWS), Steve Dodd (CDO of MAS Holdings), Jayantha Fernando (Partner, Heritage Partners), and Ishari Siriwardane (General Manager of Digital Transformation Projects at SLT-Mobitel) debated the core challenges of balancing sovereignty with speed, tackling the national tech talent bottleneck, and financing digital transformation at scale. Providing crucial context, sessions from AWS leaders Mayank Arora, leading Government, Education, and Transportation for AWS in India & South Asia, and Raja Rajaram, Hybrid Cloud Business Leader for AWS in APJ, detailed the practical path forward, sharing public sector success stories from across the region and presenting a deep dive into the AWS Outposts architecture for achieving a truly consistent hybrid cloud experience. Imal Kalutotage, Founder and Chairman of NCINGA stated, “This a historic moment for the country where a hyperscaler like AWS showing their commitment to invest in Sri Lanka. We moved beyond why we need digital transformation to how we architect it for a sovereign nation. Through our partnership with AWS and our deep commitment to Sri Lanka, NCINGA is not just providing technology; we are building the foundational pillars that will support our nation’s economic aspirations for decades to come.” The event concluded with interactive showcases of the NCINGA AI Factory and AWS Outposts, providing a tangible roadmap for how public and private sector entities can begin their modernisation journey immediately, without compromising on security or sovereignty.

results.title: PH’s upper-middle-income status: Milestone or mirage?
results.description: The Philippines has officially entered the World Bank’s upper-middle-income category, with its gross national income per capita reaching $4,850, exceeding the $4,636 threshold in the World Bank’s latest income classification. The reclassification is a statistical achievement. Upper-middle-income country, or UMIC, status can enhance investor confidence and global prestige. But the Philippines’ classification as an upper-middle-income country is also a mirage — an illusion of prosperity that masks inequality, debt dependence, declining purchasing power, debt rigidity and vulnerability to external shocks. Without structural reforms in currency stabilization, agricultural empowerment and governance accountability, the UMIC label risks becoming another layer of dependency [...]... Keep on reading: PH’s upper-middle-income status: Milestone or mirage?

results.title: Ayodhya Ram Temple Probe: SIT Expands Land Deal Investigation as Parallel Donation Theft Inquiry Continues
results.description: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) has intensified its investigation in Ayodhya by widening its probe into land purchases made by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, while a separate investigation into the alleged theft of temple offerings is also progressing simultaneously. As part of the latest phase of the inquiry, SIT officials revisited Ayodhya and examined documents related to land acquisitions carried out by the Trust. Investigators questioned Trust member Gopal Rao and trustee Dr. Anil Mishra, with the latter reportedly undergoing several hours of questioning regarding land-related records and payment procedures. Officials are reviewing documentation to verify the circumstances surrounding the transactions. According to sources, the SIT also plans to question Champat Rai, the Trust's General Secretary, to seek his response regarding allegations linked to certain land purchases. The investigation is examining claims that some properties were acquired at prices significantly higher than their reported values. Authorities are verifying documentary evidence before drawing any conclusions. The SIT is expected to submit its final report by July 15, making the ongoing phase of evidence collection and witness examination particularly significant. Investigators continue to scrutinize financial records, land documents, and statements from individuals connected to the transactions. Parallel to the land inquiry, police are continuing their investigation into the alleged theft of offerings received at the Ram Temple. The accused, Avinash Shukla, remains under interrogation as investigators pursue additional evidence, including the search for a motorcycle believed to be relevant to the case. Political reactions have also emerged following the developments, with leaders from different sides making statements over the ongoing investigations and allegations. However, the SIT has not announced any findings or established wrongdoing at this stage. Both investigations remain active, and officials have emphasized that conclusions will be based on the evidence gathered during the inquiry.

results.title: Romanian healthcare operator MedLife reaches network of 37 hyperclinics after recent Bucharest opening
results.description: Romanian private healthcare network MedLife has opened a hyperclinic in Bucharest’s District 5 following an investment of more than EUR 1 million. With this opening, MedLife now operates 37 hyperclinics nationwide, including eight in the capital. The company’s hyperclinic concept describes large-scale medical facilities that provide patients with a one-stop-shop healthcare model, offering “everything from laboratory testing and specialist consultations to diagnostic investigations and ongoing patient monitoring.” The MedLife Panduri Hyperclinic spans approximately 1,000 sqm across three floors and features 16 consultation rooms, a minor procedures room, and a dedicated biological sample collection unit. Patients have access to 27 medical specialties, including obstetrics and gynecology, internal medicine, rheumatology, diabetes, nutrition and metabolic diseases, neurology, cardiology, endocrinology, oncology, nephrology, and many others. MedLife Group, which is listed on the Bucharest Stock Exchange, also operates 94 clinics nationwide, serving both major urban centers and smaller cities. simona@romania-insider.com (Photo source: the company)

results.title: BML launches SplitPay to offer flexible instalment payments
